title: poa hackelii
intro: Poa hackelii, also known as Hackel's fescue or blue fescue, is a species of grass native to China and parts of Eastern Asia, characterized by its blue-green foliage and slender stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Poa hackelii include its ability to reduce swelling, promote wound healing, and act as an anti-bacterial agent. Herbal preparations of Poa hackelii can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts, which are often used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
